title: Linosyris parvifolia
taxonomy: Linosyris parvifolia is classified within the family Asteraceae. This classification places it among the daisy or sunflower family. Further classification includes the tribe Cichorieae and the subtribe Lactucinae. Linosyris parvifolia is native to South Africa, where it can be found in a variety of habitats. Its classification as a species of the genus Linosyris is also recognized in botanical studies.

intro: Linosyris parvifolia, also known as the lesser hawk's beard, is a plant species within the family Asteraceae. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which may contribute to its potential use in treating respiratory issues and wound healing. The bioactive constituents of Linosyris parvifolia include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
